title: ModelSessionSpan + ActivitySpan primitive types
goal: Land the typed observability span primitives required by spec v02.186 and folded WP-1-Session-Observability-Spans-FR-v1 stub intent: ModelSessionSpan binds session-wide observability via model_session_id; ActivitySpan binds bounded activity scopes (MT iteration, mailbox lease, model swap, checkpoint write) inside a session. Span bindings let session-wide queries work across multi-session swarm execution.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/observability/mod.rs + src/backend/handshake_core/src/observability/spans.rs with `ModelSessionSpan`, `ActivitySpan`, `SpanContext`, `SpanGuard`; unit tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/observability_spans_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: KERNEL-001 TraceProjection + KERNEL-002 traces already in main; this MT layers cluster X session-observability spans on top.
implementation_notes: Per folded WP-1-Session-Observability-Spans-FR-v1 intent (refinement.json line 1011): 'session-scoped observability for multi-session execution: new FR event families, base schema correlation via model_session_id, and span bindings (ModelSessionSpan + ActivitySpan).' Define `ModelSessionSpan { span_id: Uuid v7, model_session_id: Uuid v7, session_id: Uuid v7, started_at_utc, ended_at_utc: Option, attributes: BTreeMap<String, AttributeValue>, status: SpanStatus (Active|Completed|Failed { reason })}`. `ActivitySpan { span_id: Uuid v7, parent_span_id: Uuid v7 (FK to ModelSessionSpan.span_id), activity_kind: ActivityKind (MtIteration|MailboxLease|ModelSwap|CheckpointWrite|StateReplay|ToolInvocation|Other(String)), started_at_utc, ended_at_utc: Option, attributes, status }`. Use `tracing` crate (already in handshake_core dep tree) for OpenTelemetry-compatible API. `SpanGuard` is an RAII wrapper that records ended_at_utc on Drop and emits FR-EVT-SPAN-ENDED (event id resolved via MT-198 registry). `SpanContext` is the thread-local + task-local handle that nested ActivitySpans use to discover their parent. ModelSessionSpan::start(model_session_id, session_id, attributes) -> SpanGuard; ActivitySpan::start(activity_kind, attributes) -> SpanGuard (looks up parent from SpanContext). Attributes are typed via `AttributeValue { String(String), Int(i64), Float(f64), Bool(bool) }` (no nested objects — keeps wire format simple, matches OpenTelemetry attribute model). Cargo additions: tracing, tracing-opentelemetry (optional behind a feature flag for downstream wiring), uuid (already added). Tests: (a) ModelSessionSpan + 3 nested ActivitySpans, Drop order asserts FR-EVT-SPAN-ENDED in correct order; (b) attribute round-trip; (c) status transitions Active->Failed{reason} preserved across Drop; (d) span without parent ActivitySpan -> standalone ModelSessionSpan recorded.
risk_if_missed: Without typed spans, session-wide queries cannot correlate across multi-session swarms (folded stub's central pain point); multi-session orchestration becomes un-debuggable per the stub's risk statement.
validator_focus: SpanGuard Drop semantics correct (always records end time, even on panic via unwind); attribute model simple enough to serialize cleanly; parent-discovery via SpanContext task-local (not thread-local since tokio tasks migrate threads).

postgres_test_url_live_evidence_20260525.summary: Cross-MT live POSTGRES_TEST_URL continuation evidence relevant to model-session scheduling, crash-recovery/session terminal behavior, observability spans, and MT-189 orchestrator state. Supporting evidence only; Integration Validator status remains PENDING.
implementation_or_hardening_notes: Postgres session_messages.token_count now decodes nullable INT8 or INT4, closing the model_session_scheduler token_count ColumnDecode failure exposed by live Postgres.
model_run cloud-consent terminal paths persist the ModelSession state before emitting terminal session events, closing the Completed-job/Active-session split seen on the valid-bundle path.

Spawned workflow finalization errors are no longer discarded silently; if the job is still non-terminal, the node, job, and workflow run are marked failed best-effort with workflow_finalization_failed evidence.
Postgres workflow_node_executions now set finished_at for completed_with_issues and poisoned terminal statuses, with a live Postgres regression.

postgres_test_url_live_evidence_20260525.summary: Post-kbstart live POSTGRES_TEST_URL model_session_scheduler_tests proof. The exact scheduler queue path and full default-threaded test binary both pass inside 20-minute command bounds after Postgres-only harness/schema fixes and session registry/worktree hardening. Supporting evidence only; Integration Validator status remains PENDING.
implementation_or_hardening_notes: model_session_scheduler_tests now use a live Postgres-only harness with isolated schemas and focused schema installation; no SQLite harness path is used.
Focused schema includes ai_job_mcp_fields so session observability spans can persist MCP/tool-call correlation rows.
SessionRegistry keeps worktree allocations through terminal finalization so persisted ModelSession state does not regress to Blocked after completion.
Model-run dispatch locking is per SessionRegistry rather than a process-global async lock, reducing cross-test contention while preserving deterministic dispatch within a registry.
Session worktree add/remove operations are serialized around git worktree metadata.

minimum_controls: SpanGuard Drop records ended_at_utc EVEN ON PANIC (tested via std::panic::catch_unwind + scopeguard).
SpanContext uses tokio task_local! not thread_local! so async migration does not break parent discovery.
Attribute size cap (per-attribute 1024 bytes; per-span 16 attributes) enforced to prevent runaway memory.

implementation_supporting_evidence_20260.summary: Implemented missing MT-196 task-local SpanContext activity-parent discovery and span attribute caps in the existing flight_recorder::spans fold; focused and full backend testutils proof passed.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: ModelSessionSpan + ActivitySpan + SpanGuard + SpanContext (tokio task_local) land cleanly; SpanGuard Drop unconditionally records SpanEnded or SpanFailed via FrSpanRecorder (covered on panic via catch_unwind in MT-199 tests); attribute caps (16 attrs, 1024 bytes/attr) enforced.
